scores.brief_assessment: Tightly curated and predominantly novel reference covering proprietary Grove path conventions, internal RST directive types, and MongoDB docs project structure — all directly actionable for the scanning/classification phase of page-level migration, with only a minor gap in that the handoff phrase 'process each one through the remaining migration steps' is vague and assumes the agent already knows what those steps are.
scores.novel_info: The most novel details are the internal directory-path taxonomy used to classify code directives — specifically the `/code-examples/tested/`, `/includes/code-examples/`, `/code-examples/includes/`, and `/driver-examples/` path conventions that signal migration status — and the `.. io-code-block::` custom Snooty directive with its `.. input::` / `.. output::` subdirectives, which are MongoDB-specific RST extensions not found in standard Sphinx. A reviewer should also verify the claim that the MongoDB Manual current version lives at `content/manual/manual/` (not `current/`) and confirm that "Grove" supports exactly the six listed languages (C#, Go, Java, JavaScript, Mongosh, Python) with no others.
